summary: Iter 25 — Watch-Together end-to-end + DPadHint. BACKEND: 13/13 pytest tests PASS (added new tests for the two-stage play→loading→countdown handshake and ready-outside-loading guardrail). The /api/watch-party/create returns a 6-char code matching A-HJ-NP-Z2-9 alphabet, /state/{code} returns full party state for known codes and {error:'not_found'} for unknown, WebSocket /api/watch-party/ws/{code} correctly handles hello (host+guest), pick, play (→loading not countdown), ready (flips loading→countdown only when ALL members ready), pause, chat, disconnect rebroadcast. FRONTEND: /watch-together lobby renders Host/Join cards cleanly. Clicking 'Host a party' from a fresh load creates a party code (e.g. 9JYGEE), opens host view with copy button + member rail + TVKeyboard for picking a title. Direct browser-side WS smoke shows code creation + hello + joined + state broadcast in <500ms. DPadHint mounts on /, /sports, /live-tv with per-page storage key vesper-dpad-hint-views:<key>, counter bumps on each mount (2× in dev due to React.StrictMode double-mount), and hint correctly hides when counter >= MAX_VIEWS (3) — verified by setting counter to 3 and re-mounting Home (hint absent). Player.jsx ready-signal: code review confirms streamReadyRef + partyReadySentRef are wired BOTH in ws.onopen (line 490 — sends ready if buffer already arrived before WS opened) AND in a dedicated useEffect at line 646 (sends ready when streamReady fires after WS open). partyReadySentRef resets on url change so re-plays re-handshake. No regressions in SideNav dwell / BACK key (out of scope for this iter but app loads cleanly across /, /sports, /live-tv, /watch-together).

ui_bugs.issue: Saw a transient 'TypeError: Failed to execute \'json\' on \'Response\': body stream already read at startHost' once after running an in-page WebSocket smoke test via page.evaluate. NOT reproducible on a clean page load — clicking Host a party from a fresh navigation succeeded and produced code 9JYGEE. Likely a React.StrictMode dev-mode double-invocation race when other in-flight fetches share an HMR module. Recommend a defensive guard in startHost so a double-click cannot re-enter while the first request is pending (e.g. `if (creatingRef.current) return; creatingRef.current = true; try{...}finally{creatingRef.current=false}`).

action_items: Optional hardening: add a re-entrancy guard inside WatchTogether.startHost / joinAs so a double click (or React StrictMode dev-double-invoke) cannot fire two POSTs that race over the same Response body.

Consider unwrapping <React.StrictMode> only for production builds — does not affect production but causes the DPadHint counter to bump by 2 per visit in dev (still caps at MAX_VIEWS so behaviour is correct in prod where StrictMode no-ops effect doubling).

Backend reaper evicts parties with zero connected members after 5 min and any party after 6 h. Feature D (3-hour idleness with a heartbeat) is implicitly satisfied because parties live up to 6h on create_at — but there is no membership-level heartbeat; if all members disconnect briefly the room is evicted after 5 min. Consider extending the no-members threshold to 30 min if guests are expected to drop/rejoin.
critical_code_review_comments: watch_party.py:255-262 — the ready→countdown flip is gated by `len(party.members) > 0 and all(m.ready for m in party.members.values())`. This is correct, but if a NEW member joins (hello) while status=='loading', they start with ready=False which means the existing 'all ready' check would never have been true for them — but if all OTHER members had already gone ready and triggered countdown BEFORE the join, the new member would join into status='countdown' without ever sending ready. That's OK semantically (they'll just play late) but worth a comment.
watch_party.py:236-243 — 'pick' resets status to 'lobby' and clears every member's ready=False, which is correct. Same with 'play' at 282-283.
Player.jsx:490 — sending `ready` inside ws.onopen guards `if (streamReadyRef.current && !partyReadySentRef.current)`. Good — handles the race where the stream finished buffering BEFORE the WS opened.
Player.jsx:646-655 — useEffect with deps [streamReady, partyCode, url] sends `ready` exactly once per url change. partyReadySentRef gates re-entry. Good.

DPadHint.jsx:39 — `useState(() => readCount(fullKey) < MAX_VIEWS)` correctly determines visibility on mount. bumpCount runs inside useEffect, so per render with StrictMode in dev the counter increments by 2 per visit — production builds (no StrictMode) bump by 1. Behaviour caps at MAX_VIEWS either way.
DPadHint.jsx:65 — `pointerEvents: 'none'` ensures the hint never blocks D-pad spatial focus from passing through, good for TV UX.

test_watch_party.py was updated: old iter18 test `test_host_play_sets_countdown_future_at_ms` was renamed to `test_host_play_transitions_to_loading` to reflect the new 2-stage handshake contract (play → loading, NOT play → countdown).

success_rate.frontend: 95% (lobby renders, host flow works on clean load, DPadHint correct on all 3 pages; one transient 'body stream already read' seen in dev-mode after a prior in-page fetch smoke, not repro on fresh load)

test_credentials: Tester profile seeded in localStorage: onnowtv-profiles-v1 + onnowtv-active-profile-v1='tester-auto' (plain string).
seed_data_creation: None persistent — backend party rooms are ephemeral; codes generated per test (e.g. J9RFSA, 9JYGEE).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend test_watch_party.py now has 3 test classes (REST, WS, ReadyHandshake, GuestJoin = 13 tests). Always export REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL before running pytest (the test file asserts it). The 2-stage handshake is: pick → play (status='loading', at_ms=0) → each member sends {type:'ready', member_id:<id>} → when ALL members have ready=True, server flips to status='countdown' with at_ms=now+pending_lead_ms. Frontend ready signal lives in Player.jsx (NOT WatchTogether.jsx). For UI regression, watch out for React.StrictMode dev double-invoke of effects (causes DPadHint counter to bump by 2 per visit in dev — harmless, capped at MAX_VIEWS).
rca of the issue: N/A — no critical issues found. Transient 'body stream already read' from startHost was a one-off in dev mode after a prior in-page page.evaluate fetch smoke test — could not be reproduced on a clean navigation. Likely cause: React.StrictMode dev-mode invariant where a stale closure from a prior render re-runs an onClick handler whose response was already consumed. Recommended defensive fix in action_items (re-entrancy guard).
